constructs: counterfactual thinking, self-esteem protection, motivation reversal, excuse-generating attribution
claim: The same upward counterfactual thought generates opposite affective and behavioral outcomes depending on whether the individual prioritizes self-protection or performance improvement.

constructs: cognitive accessibility, aversive imagination, heuristic judgment, dual-process cognition
claim: Spontaneous mental accessibility of risky outcomes drives inflated likelihood estimates as a byproduct of negativity bias and imagination fluency, not because outcomes are objectively more probable.

phenomenon: perpetuation of insecurity through expressed vulnerability
constructs: vulnerability expression, reflected appraisals, authenticity doubts, egocentric bias, interpersonal cycles
claim: Expressing relational insecurity initiates a cognitive feedback loop in which perceived vulnerability triggers doubts about partner authenticity, which then verify the insecurity that prompted the expression.

constructs: color perception, sexual signaling, implicit bias, automatic evaluation, biological priming
claim: Red color carries evolved sexual meaning that automatically amplifies attractiveness and sexual desire in men via semantic activation, bypassing conscious awareness.

constructs: attractions-barriers-alternatives, contextual stress, adaptation capacity, feedback loops, equilibrium dynamics
claim: Marital outcome trajectories reflect ongoing dynamic equilibrium between competing forces; couples with identical initial dissatisfaction may follow divergent paths based on timing and magnitude of stressors relative to adaptive capacity.

phenomenon: personality-mediated affect regulation and life satisfaction
constructs: temperamental traits, instrumental trait-enactment, domain satisfaction aggregation, emotional baseline stability
claim: Personality traits generate well-being through distinct pathways: stable temperamental dispositions set emotional baseline, while behavioral traits shape the frequency of rewarding life experiences that feed longer-term satisfaction.
